channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Outside 64, 27th April
item.description:
The hole outside 64 is quite deep and wide spilling gravel everywhere

Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown hedges/trees, 27th April
item.description:
The bus stop known as Ermin Park is obscured from view, and the bus drivers are unable to see the bus stop and often over shoot it when I want to get off.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ermin Street, Brockworth, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: No maintenance done on stream by the road, now pouring down the road to the culvert at my property eroding tarmac and verge., 27th April
item.description:
Above The Mill Mill Lane Prestbury GL523NF

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Upper Mill Lane, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street sign vandalised, missing, 27th April
item.description:
The sign is missing by Greenacre, people are unable to find the house
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 51, New Barn Lane, Marle Hill, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Signs & Barriers left behind by Contractor

item.title: Potential Need for Double Yellows, 27th April
item.description:
As you turn off Shurdington Rd into Mill Lane, there are parked cars on the left hand side of the road which are difficult/risky to pass when traffic is coming towards Shurdington Rd. After the turning into the Brockworth House (Care Centre), it becomes too narrow for two way traffic and can cause backlog preventing traffic getting into Mill Lane, particularly when the school buses or refuge lorries are coming in. As you get closer to the Jct for Lavender Avenue and the road veers to the left, the parked cars obscure almost all visibility of oncoming traffic whilst having to overtake said parked cars. I have regularly used this road for the last 18 months and have had a number of close calls and avoidable traffic situations so wanted to highlight this as a safety issue that should be resolved with double yellows to prevent these cars from parking in this section. There is a specific small red car (C1/107) that is almost always parked opposite Lavender Avenue which shouldn't be parking there from a highway code perspective also.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 78, Mill Lane, Brockworth, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Volunteer litter picker, 27th April
item.description:
I have left a bag of recycling rubbish by bin ref:- 0230, which is by the traffic lights at the Grayhound pub.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Greyhound Gardens, Elmbridge, Gloucester
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Litter picking bags ready for collection

item.title: There are 2 badly degraded sections of pavement on Glenfall Way close to 1 Warwick Crescent, 27th April
item.description:
There are 2 badly degraded sections of pavement on Glenfall Way close to 1 Warwick Crescent. They cause problems for pedestrians, in particular people with mobility issues.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Glenfall Way, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Other damage to pavement i.e. cobbles, raised mound/surface

item.title: 4 or 5 reasonably deep holes running along the front of my front plinth, 27th April
item.description:
328 Prestbury Road The holes allow dirt to collect which then causes weeds and grass to grow, I’d try to repair myself but I understand I’m prohibited I think the path is deteriorating over time I appreciate you’re probably very busy but if there’s any tarmac spare after pothole repairs I’d appreciate it

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 325, Prestbury Road, Lynworth, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Post or sign leaning slightly (less than 20 degrees) problem, 27th April
item.description:
PERM Could you raise a perm repair enquiry for the above defect ( bit of a funny one) although no one knows where the sign has come from a resident found it outside her property. Many thanks
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Post or sign leaning slightly (less than 20 degrees)

item.title: Unpermitted roadworks problem, 27th April
item.description:
Customer reports that they are under pressure to employ a contractor to paint a fence at the location, which faces onto the footway. Recently, marks have appeared which suggest that works are planned [No permit showing for next 12 months]. They are concerned about starting work and there being a conflict, as they'll need access. They are prepared to defer their plans, but ask when it will be okay to proceed: when are the works planned for and their duration? They also query that, should they start and a team arrives on site, would there be any liability on their part and would they have to stop?
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street light is permanently out problem, 27th April
item.description:
Problem with 2 Lampposts. 1 is one and 1 is continually flashing from when they come on in the evening until they go off in the morning. This is bad for anyone with photo-sensitivity.
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: TMC - Defects - Vegetation problem, 27th April
item.description:
Fell dead bean tree to 1m stump hight. No parking cone will be required (Damaged Tree)
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree root bringing up pavement and damaging wall, 27th April
item.description:
The pavement area that is being damaged by the tree root is outside Homespa House which is number 37 Christchurch Road. The root is causing damage to the wall of the property so needs sorting.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 37, Christchurch Road, Lansdown, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Other damage to pavement i.e. cobbles, raised mound/surface

item.title: A417 diversion route sign blocking visibility out of Old Gloucester Road onto the A4136, 27th April
item.description:
There is a diversion sign blocking the left hand junction visibility from Old Gloucester Road for vehicles turning right onto the A4136.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): A436, Cotswold
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Signs & Barriers left behind by Contractor

item.title: Road manholes loosened after work creating noise, 27th April

O/s church Clarence Street Cheltenham
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Crescent Place, Ewens Farm, Cheltenham

item.category: Signs & Barriers left behind by Contractor

item.title: Roadworks blocking pavement and cycleway, 27th April
item.description:
These roadworks are completely blocking the pedestrian and cycle rights of way. There should be a way through protected by barriers.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 303, Gloucester Road, Hatherley, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Obstruction by unpermitted skip, scaffolding, building material on pavement

item.title: A number of large golf ball depth and 300 mm wide holes in the road by the layby., 27th April
item.description:
The holes are next to the bridlepath and layby. Some of the holes have been filled in a few years back, but have now re-opened. They are scattered across the whole road, which means you have to drive over some, which just makes them worse.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cotswold
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bin bag wrapped item, 27th April
item.description:
Can’t identify what it is but it’s like a stick object wrapped in a tied bin bag.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Brionne Way, Longlevens, Gloucester
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Flytipping (not witnessed and no evidence likely)

item.title: The drain is blocked problem, 27th April
item.description:
Customer reports that the drain cover at the location is "clacking" and keeping them awake at night. This has been reported on multiple occasions previously, but no action has been forthcoming. They believe that the drain itself is blocked with tarmac and rubble, which is causing the road to flood in inclement weather.
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: A pothole in road problem, 27th April
item.description:
Please could you send me a Highways Incident Report form please in relation to pot hole damage caused on 23/4/26 at 3pm. The pothole was located on the road leading from the A345 to Cowley Manor, just outside Park Farm. The what3words location is ///fidgeted.bravery.flop The defect measures approximately 90cm by 45cm and was around 10cm deep. This size presents a clear hazard to road users and exceeds typical intervention thresholds for repair. Importantly, the pothole had been marked with a white box, which appeared faded at the time of the incident. This indicates that the defect had already been identified prior to my accident but had not been repaired within a reasonable timeframe. As a result of striking the pothole, my vehicle sustained damage to a tyre, which required repair/replacement. Please find attached: • Photographs of the pothole, including the faded road marking • Photographs of the vehicle damage • Receipt/invoice for the repair/replacement I would be grateful if you could: 1. Confirm receipt of this claim 2. Provide details of inspection and maintenance records for this location 3. Advise on the next steps in progressing my claim I look forward to your response. Reference: for claim - 70012772
Report on FixMyStreet
